description:
You are an edge-computing engineer getting a batch of IoT sensors ready for an
over-the-air (OTA) firmware push.  
Inside the workspace there is already a comma-separated inventory file located at  
/home/user/iot_devices/device_registry.csv

Your goal is to do three things with nothing but awk and/or sed (you may
pipeline them together or run them as separate commands, but do not rely on any
other text-processing utilities):

1. Generate /home/user/iot_devices/device_registry_updated.csv  
   • Keep the header row exactly as-is.  
   • For every data row whose firmware column equals exactly “1.2.3”, change the
     value to “1.3.0”.  
   • Leave all other rows untouched.  
   • The field order, delimiter, and line ordering must stay exactly the same as
     the original file.  
   • Make sure the file ends with a single trailing newline character.

2. Produce /home/user/iot_devices/updated_macs.txt  
   • This file must list only the MAC addresses (second column in the CSV)
     belonging to the devices whose firmware was changed to “1.3.0”.  
   • One MAC address per line, no extra whitespace, and the file must also end
     with a single trailing newline.

3. Create a two-line log file named /home/user/iot_devices/update_summary.log  
   • It must contain *exactly* the two lines below (no leading/trailing
     spaces).  
       Total devices processed: 5  
       Devices updated: 3  
   • Each of the two lines must end with a newline character.

Important verification requirements  
• Only awk and/or sed may be used.  
• All three output files must be created in /home/user/iot_devices/.  
• Filenames and letter casing must match exactly.  
• Automated tests will open each output file byte-for-byte; even an extra
  newline or space will cause failure, so pay close attention to formatting.

Hint: piping the output of sed into awk (or vice-versa) counts as a single
pipeline, so you can complete everything in as little as three or four commands.
truth:
Initial file (pre-existing when the agent starts)
/home/user/iot_devices/device_registry.csv
------------------------------------------------
device_id,mac_addr,location,firmware
dev-001,AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01,warehouse,1.2.3
dev-002,AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:02,warehouse,1.2.2
dev-003,AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:03,retail_store,1.2.3
dev-004,AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:04,office,1.2.1
dev-005,AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:05,office,1.2.3␊   ← single terminating newline

Expected file after successful completion
/home/user/iot_devices/device_registry_updated.csv
--------------------------------------------------
device_id,mac_addr,location,firmware
dev-001,AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01,warehouse,1.3.0
dev-002,AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:02,warehouse,1.2.2
dev-003,AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:03,retail_store,1.3.0
dev-004,AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:04,office,1.2.1
dev-005,AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:05,office,1.3.0␊   ← single terminating newline

Expected file
/home/user/iot_devices/updated_macs.txt
---------------------------------------
AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:01
AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:03
AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:05␊

Expected file
/home/user/iot_devices/update_summary.log
-----------------------------------------
Total devices processed: 5
Devices updated: 3␊
name: task_000000_520cff0b
